description_long: This is a traditional European-style windmill with a tall, octagonal stone tower and a wooden shingle roof. Four large wooden lattice blades are mounted on a central axis at the front, extending outward in a cross pattern. The tower features small rectangular windows and a wooden door at the base, suggesting interior access. The structure sits on a slightly raised, dark stone foundation, providing stability. The woodwork of the roof and blades is visibly textured, contrasting with the rough, whitewashed stone of the tower. This windmill would typically be found in parks, rural-urban edges, or as a historical landmark within a city.
description: A tall, octagonal stone windmill with a wooden shingle roof and four large wooden lattice blades, featuring small windows and a wooden door at the base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the four wooden blades, central axis, and the main entrance door at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the octagonal stone tower, shingle roof, and the profile of the windmill blades.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the tower with two windows and the wooden roof, with the blades seen edge-on.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the stone tower, roof, and the side profile of the windmill blades.
